title: malva sylvestris subsp. subacaulis
intro: Malva sylvestris subsp. subacaulis, a subspecies of the Malva sylvestris plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, pinkish flowers and roundish leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties, which are attributed to its high mucilage content. The health benefits of Malva sylvestris subsp. subacaulis include relief from digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the production of skincare products and pharmaceuticals.
